<a name="Behavior"></a>

## type [Behavior](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L54>)

Behavior is an alias for \[tw.Behavior\] to configure table behavior settings. This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.

Deprecated: Use \[tw.Behavior\] directly to configure settings such as auto\-hiding empty columns, trimming spaces, or controlling header/footer visibility.

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
var b tablewriter.Behavior = tablewriter.Behavior{AutoHide: tw.On}
// New (recommended)
var b tw.Behavior = tw.Behavior{AutoHide: tw.On}
```

```go
type Behavior tw.Behavior
```

```go
}
```

FooterFormattingBuilder configures footer formatting

```go
type FooterFormattingBuilder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FooterFormattingBuilder.Build"></a>

### func \(\*FooterFormattingBuilder\) [Build](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/config.go#L564>)

```go
func (ff *FooterFormattingBuilder) Build() *FooterConfigBuilder
```

Build returns the parent FooterConfigBuilder

<a name="FooterFormattingBuilder.WithAlignment"></a>

### func \(\*FooterFormattingBuilder\) [WithAlignment](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L128>)

```go
func (ff *FooterFormattingBuilder) WithAlignment(align tw.Align) *FooterFormattingBuilder
```

WithAlignment sets the text alignment for footer cells within the formatting configuration. This method is deprecated and will be removed in the next version.

Deprecated: Use [FooterConfigBuilder.Alignment](<#FooterConfigBuilder.Alignment>) with [AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithGlobal](<#AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithGlobal>) or [AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithPerColumn](<#AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithPerColumn>) to configure footer alignments. Alternatively, apply a complete \[tw.CellAlignment\] configuration using [WithFooterAlignmentConfig](<#WithFooterAlignmentConfig>).

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
builder.Footer().Formatting().WithAlignment(tw.AlignRight)
// New (recommended)
builder.Footer().Alignment().WithGlobal(tw.AlignRight)
// Or
table.Options(WithFooterAlignmentConfig(tw.CellAlignment{Global: tw.AlignRight}))
```

Parameters:

- align: The \[tw.Align\] value to set for footer cells. Valid values are \[tw.AlignLeft\], \[tw.AlignRight\], \[tw.AlignCenter\], and \[tw.AlignNone\]. Invalid alignments are ignored.

Returns:

```
The [FooterFormattingBuilder] instance for method chaining.
```

## type [HeaderFormattingBuilder](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/config.go#L487-L491>)

HeaderFormattingBuilder configures header formatting

```go
type HeaderFormattingBuilder struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="HeaderFormattingBuilder.Build"></a>

### func \(\*HeaderFormattingBuilder\) [Build](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/config.go#L494>)

```go
func (hf *HeaderFormattingBuilder) Build() *HeaderConfigBuilder
```

Build returns the parent HeaderConfigBuilder

<a name="HeaderFormattingBuilder.WithAlignment"></a>

### func \(\*HeaderFormattingBuilder\) [WithAlignment](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L161>)

```go
func (hf *HeaderFormattingBuilder) WithAlignment(align tw.Align) *HeaderFormattingBuilder
```

WithAlignment sets the text alignment for header cells within the formatting configuration. This method is deprecated and will be removed in the next version.

Deprecated: Use [HeaderConfigBuilder.Alignment](<#HeaderConfigBuilder.Alignment>) with [AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithGlobal](<#AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithGlobal>) or [AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithPerColumn](<#AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithPerColumn>) to configure header alignments. Alternatively, apply a complete \[tw.CellAlignment\] configuration using [WithHeaderAlignmentConfig](<#WithHeaderAlignmentConfig>).

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
builder.Header().Formatting().WithAlignment(tw.AlignCenter)
// New (recommended)
builder.Header().Alignment().WithGlobal(tw.AlignCenter)
// Or
table.Options(WithHeaderAlignmentConfig(tw.CellAlignment{Global: tw.AlignCenter}))
```

Parameters:

- align: The \[tw.Align\] value to set for header cells. Valid values are \[tw.AlignLeft\], \[tw.AlignRight\], \[tw.AlignCenter\], and \[tw.AlignNone\]. Invalid alignments are ignored.

Returns:

```
The [HeaderFormattingBuilder] instance for method chaining.
```


WithAutoFormat enables/disables auto formatting

<a name="Option"></a>

## type [Option](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L13>)

Option defines a function type for configuring a Table instance.

```go
type Option func(target *Table)
```

<a name="WithAlignment"></a>

### func [WithAlignment](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L610>)

```go
func WithAlignment(alignment tw.Alignment) Option
```

WithAlignment sets the default column alignment for the header, rows, and footer. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithAutoHide"></a>

### func [WithAutoHide](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L17>)

```go
func WithAutoHide(state tw.State) Option
```

WithAutoHide enables or disables automatic hiding of columns with empty data rows. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithBehavior"></a>

### func [WithBehavior](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L623>)

```go
func WithBehavior(behavior tw.Behavior) Option
```

WithBehavior applies a behavior configuration to the table. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithBorders"></a>

### func [WithBorders](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L30>)

```go
func WithBorders(borders tw.Border) Option
```

WithBorders configures the table's border settings by updating the renderer's border configuration. This function is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.

Deprecated: Use [WithRendition](<#WithRendition>) to configure border settings for renderers that support \[tw.Renditioning\], or update the renderer's \[tw.RenderConfig\] directly via its Config\(\) method. This function has no effect if no renderer is set on the table.

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
table.Options(WithBorders(tw.Border{Top: true, Bottom: true}))
// New (recommended)
table.Options(WithRendition(tw.Rendition{Borders: tw.Border{Top: true, Bottom: true}}))
```

Parameters:

- borders: The \[tw.Border\] configuration to apply to the renderer's borders.

Returns:

```
An [Option] that updates the renderer's border settings if a renderer is set.
Logs a debug message if debugging is enabled and a renderer is present.
```

<a name="WithColumnMax"></a>

### func [WithColumnMax](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L28>)

```go
func WithColumnMax(width int) Option
```

WithColumnMax sets a global maximum column width for the table in streaming mode. Negative values are ignored, and the change is logged if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithColumnWidths"></a>

### func [WithColumnWidths](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L67>)

```go
func WithColumnWidths(widths tw.Mapper[int, int]) Option
```

WithColumnWidths sets per\-column widths for the table. Negative widths are removed, and the change is logged if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithCondition"></a>

### func [WithCondition](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L231>)

```go
func WithCondition(cond *runewidth.Condition) Option
```

Deprecated: use WithEastAsian instead. WithCondition provides a way to set a custom global runewidth.Condition that will be used for all subsequent display width calculations by the twwidth \(twdw\) package.

The runewidth.Condition object allows for more fine\-grained control over how rune widths are determined, beyond just toggling EastAsianWidth. This could include settings for ambiguous width characters or other future properties of runewidth.Condition.

<a name="WithConfig"></a>

### func [WithConfig](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L82>)

```go
func WithConfig(cfg Config) Option
```

WithConfig applies a custom configuration to the table by merging it with the default configuration.

<a name="WithCounters"></a>

### func [WithCounters](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L711>)

```go
func WithCounters(counters ...tw.Counter) Option
```

WithCounters enables line counting by wrapping the table's writer. If a custom counter \(that implements tw.Counter\) is provided, it will be used. If the provided counter is nil, a default tw.LineCounter will be used. The final count can be retrieved via the table.Lines\(\) method after Render\(\) is called.

<a name="WithDebug"></a>

### func [WithDebug](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L90>)

```go
func WithDebug(debug bool) Option
```

WithDebug enables or disables debug logging and adjusts the logger level accordingly. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithEastAsian"></a>

### func [WithEastAsian](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L675>)

```go
func WithEastAsian(state tw.State) Option
```

WithEastAsian configures the global East Asian width calculation setting.

- state=tw.On: Enables East Asian width calculations. CJK and ambiguous characters are typically measured as double width.
- state=tw.Off: Disables East Asian width calculations. Characters are generally measured as single width, subject to Unicode standards.

This setting affects all subsequent display width calculations using the twdw package.

```go

```

WithFooter sets the table footers by calling the Footer method.

<a name="WithRendererSettings"></a>

### func [WithRendererSettings](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L91>)

```go
func WithRendererSettings(settings tw.Settings) Option
```

WithRendererSettings updates the renderer's settings, such as separators and line styles. This function is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.

Deprecated: Use [WithRendition](<#WithRendition>) to update renderer settings for renderers that implement \[tw.Renditioning\], or configure the renderer's \[tw.Settings\] directly via its \[tw.Renderer.Config\] method. This function has no effect if no renderer is set.

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
table.Options(WithRendererSettings(tw.Settings{Separator: "|"}))
// New (recommended)
table.Options(WithRendition(tw.Rendition{Settings: tw.Settings{Separator: "|"}}))
```

Parameters:

- settings: The \[tw.Settings\] configuration to apply to the renderer.

Returns:

```
An [Option] that updates the renderer's settings if a renderer is set.
Logs a debug message if debugging is enabled and a renderer is present.
```

<a name="WithRendition"></a>

### func [WithRendition](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L649>)

```go
func WithRendition(rendition tw.Rendition) Option
```

WithRendition allows updating the active renderer's rendition configuration by merging the provided rendition. If the renderer does not implement tw.Renditioning, a warning is logged. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

```go
func WithRowAutoFormat(state tw.State) Option
```

WithRowAutoFormat enables or disables automatic formatting for row cells. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithRowAutoWrap"></a>

### func [WithRowAutoWrap](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L314>)

```go
func WithRowAutoWrap(wrap int) Option
```

WithRowAutoWrap sets the wrapping behavior for row cells. Invalid wrap modes are ignored, and the change is logged if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithRowCallbacks"></a>

### func [WithRowCallbacks](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L354>)

```go
func WithRowCallbacks(callbacks tw.CellCallbacks) Option
```

WithRowCallbacks sets the callback configuration for row cells. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithRowConfig"></a>

```go
func WithRowConfig(config tw.CellConfig) Option
```

WithRowConfig applies a full row configuration to the table. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithRowFilter"></a>

### func [WithRowFilter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L343>)

```go
func WithRowFilter(filter tw.CellFilter) Option
```

WithRowFilter sets the filter configuration for row cells. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithRowMaxWidth"></a>

```go
func WithRowMaxWidth(maxWidth int) Option
```

WithRowMaxWidth sets the maximum content width for row cells. Negative values are ignored, and the change is logged if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithRowMergeMode"></a>

```go
func WithRowMergeMode(mergeMode int) Option
```

Deprecated: Use a ConfigBuilder with .Row\(\).CellMerging\(\).WithMode\(...\) instead. This option will be removed in a future version.

<a name="WithRowPaddingPerColumn"></a>

### func [WithRowPaddingPerColumn](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L365>)

```go
func WithRowPaddingPerColumn(padding []tw.Padding) Option
```

WithRowPaddingPerColumn sets per\-column padding for row cells. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithStreaming"></a>

### func [WithStreaming](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L460>)

```go
func WithStreaming(c tw.StreamConfig) Option
```

WithStreaming applies a streaming configuration to the table by merging it with the existing configuration. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithStringer"></a>

### func [WithStringer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L471>)

```go
func WithStringer(stringer interface{}) Option
```

WithStringer sets a custom stringer function for converting row data and clears the stringer cache. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithStringerCache"></a>

### func [WithStringerCache](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L483>)

```go
func WithStringerCache() Option
```

WithStringerCache enables the default LRU caching for the stringer function. It initializes the cache with a default capacity if one does not already exist.

<a name="WithStringerCacheCustom"></a>

### func [WithStringerCacheCustom](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L501>)

```go
func WithStringerCacheCustom(cache twcache.Cache[reflect.Type, reflect.Value]) Option
```

WithStringerCacheCustom enables caching for the stringer function using a specific implementation. Passing nil disables caching entirely.

<a name="WithSymbols"></a>

### func [WithSymbols](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L689>)

```go
func WithSymbols(symbols tw.Symbols) Option
```

WithSymbols sets the symbols used for drawing table borders and separators. The symbols are applied to the table's renderer configuration, if a renderer is set. If no renderer is set \(target.renderer is nil\), this option has no effect. .

<a name="WithTableMax"></a>

### func [WithTableMax](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L212>)

```go
func WithTableMax(width int) Option
```

WithTableMax sets the maximum width of the entire table in characters. Negative values are ignored, and the change is logged if debugging is enabled. The width constrains the table's rendering, potentially causing text wrapping or truncation based on the configuration's wrapping settings \(e.g., tw.WrapTruncate\). If debug logging is enabled via WithDebug\(true\), the applied width is logged.

Deprecated: Use WithMaxWidth instead, which provides the same functionality with a clearer name and consistent naming across the package. For example:

```
tablewriter.NewTable(os.Stdout, tablewriter.WithMaxWidth(80))
```

<a name="WithTrimLine"></a>

### func [WithTrimLine](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L544>)

```go
func WithTrimLine(state tw.State) Option
```

WithTrimLine sets whether empty visual lines within a cell are trimmed. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithTrimSpace"></a>

### func [WithTrimSpace](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L522>)

```go
func WithTrimSpace(state tw.State) Option
```

WithTrimSpace sets whether leading and trailing spaces are automatically trimmed. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithTrimTab"></a>

### func [WithTrimTab](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L533>)

```go
func WithTrimTab(state tw.State) Option
```

WithTrimTab sets whether leading and trailing tab characters are automatically trimmed. Logs the change if debugging is enabled.

<a name="WithWidths"></a>

### func [WithWidths](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/option.go#L56>)

```go
func WithWidths(width tw.CellWidth) Option
```

WithWidths sets per\-column widths for the table. Negative widths are removed, and the change is logged if debugging is enabled.

<a name="RowFormattingBuilder.Build"></a>

```go
func (rf *RowFormattingBuilder) Build() *RowConfigBuilder
```

<a name="RowFormattingBuilder.WithAlignment"></a>

### func \(\*RowFormattingBuilder\) [WithAlignment](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L194>)

```go
func (rf *RowFormattingBuilder) WithAlignment(align tw.Align) *RowFormattingBuilder
```

WithAlignment sets the text alignment for row cells within the formatting configuration. This method is deprecated and will be removed in the next version.

Deprecated: Use [RowConfigBuilder.Alignment](<#RowConfigBuilder.Alignment>) with [AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithGlobal](<#AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithGlobal>) or [AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithPerColumn](<#AlignmentConfigBuilder.WithPerColumn>) to configure row alignments. Alternatively, apply a complete \[tw.CellAlignment\] configuration using [WithRowAlignmentConfig](<#WithRowAlignmentConfig>).

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
builder.Row().Formatting().WithAlignment(tw.AlignLeft)
// New (recommended)
builder.Row().Alignment().WithGlobal(tw.AlignLeft)
// Or
table.Options(WithRowAlignmentConfig(tw.CellAlignment{Global: tw.AlignLeft}))
```

Parameters:

- align: The \[tw.Align\] value to set for row cells. Valid values are \[tw.AlignLeft\], \[tw.AlignRight\], \[tw.AlignCenter\], and \[tw.AlignNone\]. Invalid alignments are ignored.

Returns:

```
The [RowFormattingBuilder] instance for method chaining.
```


WithAutoFormat enables/disables auto formatting

<a name="Settings"></a>

## type [Settings](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/deprecated.go#L68>)

Settings is an alias for \[tw.Settings\] to configure renderer settings. This type is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.

Deprecated: Use \[tw.Settings\] directly to configure renderer settings, such as separators and line styles.

Example migration:

```
// Old (deprecated)
var s tablewriter.Settings = tablewriter.Settings{Separator: "|"}
// New (recommended)
var s tw.Settings = tw.Settings{Separator: "|"}
```

```go
type Settings tw.Settings
```

<a name="Table"></a>

## type [Table](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L23-L59>)

Table represents a table instance with content and rendering capabilities.

```go
type Table struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCSV"></a>

### func [NewCSV](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/csv.go#L11>)

```go
func NewCSV(writer io.Writer, fileName string, hasHeader bool, opts ...Option) (*Table, error)
```

NewCSV Start A new table by importing from a CSV file Takes io.Writer and csv File name

<a name="NewCSVReader"></a>

### func [NewCSVReader](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/csv.go#L30>)

```go
func NewCSVReader(writer io.Writer, csvReader *csv.Reader, hasHeader bool, opts ...Option) (*Table, error)
```

NewCSVReader Start a New Table Writer with csv.Reader This enables customisation such as reader.Comma = ';' See http://golang.org/src/pkg/encoding/csv/reader.go?s=3213:3671#L94

<a name="NewTable"></a>

### func [NewTable](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L106>)

```go
func NewTable(w io.Writer, opts ...Option) *Table
```

NewTable creates a new table instance with specified writer and options. Parameters include writer for output and optional configuration options. Returns a pointer to the initialized Table instance.

<a name="NewWriter"></a>

### func [NewWriter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L139>)

```go
func NewWriter(w io.Writer) *Table
```

NewWriter creates a new table with default settings for backward compatibility. It logs the creation if debugging is enabled.

<a name="Table.Append"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Append](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L181>)

```go
func (t *Table) Append(rows ...interface{}) error
```

Append adds data to the current row being built for the table. This method always contributes to a single logical row in the table. To add multiple distinct rows, call Append multiple times \(once for each row's data\) or use the Bulk\(\) method if providing a slice where each element is a row.

<a name="Table.Bulk"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Bulk](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L235>)

```go
func (t *Table) Bulk(rows interface{}) error
```

Bulk adds multiple rows from a slice to the table. If Behavior.AutoHeader is enabled, no headers set, and rows is a slice of structs, automatically extracts/sets headers from the first struct.

<a name="Table.Caption"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Caption](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L150>)

```go
func (t *Table) Caption(caption tw.Caption) *Table
```

Caption sets the table caption \(legacy method\). Defaults to BottomCenter alignment, wrapping to table width. Use SetCaptionOptions for more control.

<a name="Table.Close"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Close](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/stream.go#L14>)

```go
func (t *Table) Close() error
```

Close finalizes the table stream. It requires the stream to be started \(by calling NewStreamTable\). It calls the renderer's Close method to render final elements \(like the bottom border\) and close the stream.

<a name="Table.Config"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Config](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L267>)

```go
func (t *Table) Config() Config
```

Config returns the current table configuration. No parameters are required. Returns the Config struct with current settings.

<a name="Table.Configure"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Configure](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L274>)

```go
func (t *Table) Configure(fn func(cfg *Config)) *Table
```

Configure updates the table's configuration using a provided function. Parameter fn is a function that modifies the Config struct. Returns the Table instance for method chaining.

<a name="Table.Counters"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Counters](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L554>)

```go
func (t *Table) Counters() []tw.Counter
```

Counters returns the slice of all active counter instances. This is useful when multiple counters are enabled. It must be called \*after\* Render\(\).

<a name="Table.Debug"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Debug](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L291>)

```go
func (t *Table) Debug() *bytes.Buffer
```

Debug retrieves the accumulated debug trace logs. No parameters are required. Returns a slice of debug messages including renderer logs.

<a name="Table.Footer"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Footer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L359>)

```go
func (t *Table) Footer(elements ...any)
```

Footer sets the table's footer content, padding to match column count. Parameter footers is a slice of strings for footer content. No return value. Footer sets the table's footer content. Parameter footers is a slice of strings for footer content. In streaming mode, this processes and stores the footer for rendering by Close\(\).

<a name="Table.Header"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Header](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L299>)

```go
func (t *Table) Header(elements ...any)
```

Header sets the table's header content, padding to match column count. Parameter elements is a slice of strings for header content. No return value. In streaming mode, this processes and renders the header immediately.

<a name="Table.Lines"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Lines](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L541>)

```go
func (t *Table) Lines() int
```

Lines returns the total number of lines rendered. This method is only effective if the WithLineCounter\(\) option was used during table initialization and must be called \*after\* Render\(\). It actively searches for the default tw.LineCounter among all active counters. It returns \-1 if the line counter was not enabled.

<a name="Table.Logger"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Logger](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L798>)

```go
func (t *Table) Logger() *ll.Logger
```

Logger retrieves the table's logger instance. No parameters are required. Returns the ll.Logger instance used for debug tracing.

<a name="Table.Options"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Options](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L410>)

```go
func (t *Table) Options(opts ...Option) *Table
```

Options updates the table's Options using a provided function. Parameter opts is a function that modifies the Table struct. Returns the Table instance for method chaining.

<a name="Table.Render"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Render](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L532>)

```go
func (t *Table) Render() error
```

Render triggers the table rendering process to the configured writer. No parameters are required. Returns an error if rendering fails.

<a name="Table.Renderer"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Renderer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L805>)

```go
func (t *Table) Renderer() tw.Renderer
```

Renderer retrieves the current renderer instance used by the table. No parameters are required. Returns the tw.Renderer interface instance.

<a name="Table.Reset"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Reset](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L476>)

```go
func (t *Table) Reset()
```

Reset clears all data \(headers, rows, footers, caption\) and rendering state from the table, allowing the Table instance to be reused for a new table with the same configuration and writer. It does NOT reset the configuration itself \(set by NewTable options or Configure\) or the underlying io.Writer.

<a name="Table.Start"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Start](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/stream.go#L80>)

```go
func (t *Table) Start() error
```

Start initializes the table stream. In this streaming model, renderer.Start\(\) is primarily called in NewStreamTable. This method serves as a safeguard or point for adding pre\-rendering logic. Start initializes the table stream. It is the entry point for streaming mode. Requires t.config.Stream.Enable to be true. Returns an error if streaming is disabled or the renderer does not support streaming, or if called multiple times on the same stream.

<a name="Table.Trimmer"></a>

### func \(\*Table\) [Trimmer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tablewriter.go#L566>)

```go
func (t *Table) Trimmer(str string) string
```

Trimmer trims whitespace from a string based on the Table’s configuration. It conditionally applies trimming based on TrimSpace and TrimTab settings.

Behavior Matrix: \- TrimSpace=On, TrimTab=On: Uses strings.TrimSpace \(removes all Unicode space including \\t\). \- TrimSpace=On, TrimTab=Off: Removes spaces/newlines but PRESERVES tabs. \- TrimSpace=Off, TrimTab=On: Removes only tabs. \- TrimSpace=Off, TrimTab=Off: Returns string unchanged.

<a name="ColorizedConfig"></a>

## type [ColorizedConfig](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/colorized.go#L15-L24>)

ColorizedConfig holds configuration for the Colorized table renderer.

```go
type ColorizedConfig struct {
    Borders   tw.Border   // Border visibility settings
    Settings  tw.Settings // Rendering behavior settings (e.g., separators, whitespace)
    Header    Tint        // Colors for header cells
    Column    Tint        // Colors for row cells
    Footer    Tint        // Colors for footer cells
    Border    Tint        // Colors for borders and lines
    Separator Tint        // Colors for column separators
    Symbols   tw.Symbols  // Symbols for table drawing (e.g., corners, lines)
}
```

Colors is a slice of color attributes for use with fatih/color, such as color.FgWhite or color.Bold.

```go
type Colors []color.Attribute
```

<a name="HTMLConfig"></a>

## type [HTMLConfig](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/html.go#L16-L26>)

HTMLConfig defines settings for the HTML table renderer.

```go
type HTMLConfig struct {
    EscapeContent  bool   // Whether to escape cell content
    AddLinesTag    bool   // Whether to wrap multiline content in <lines> tags
    TableClass     string // CSS class for <table>
    HeaderClass    string // CSS class for <thead>
    BodyClass      string // CSS class for <tbody>
    FooterClass    string // CSS class for <tfoot>
    RowClass       string // CSS class for <tr> in body
    HeaderRowClass string // CSS class for <tr> in header
    FooterRowClass string // CSS class for <tr> in footer
}
```

<a name="SVG"></a>

## type [SVG](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L40-L56>)

SVG implements tw.Renderer for SVG output. Manages SVG element generation and merge tracking.

```go
type SVG struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewSVG"></a>

### func [NewSVG](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L67>)

```go
func NewSVG(configs ...SVGConfig) *SVG
```

NewSVG creates a new SVG renderer with configuration. Parameter configs provides optional SVGConfig; defaults used if empty. Returns a configured SVG instance.

<a name="SVG.Close"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Close](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L285>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Close() error
```

Close finalizes SVG rendering and writes output. Parameter w is the output w. Returns an error if writing fails.

<a name="SVG.Config"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Config](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L370>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Config() tw.Rendition
```

Config returns the renderer's configuration. No parameters are required. Returns a Rendition with border and debug settings.

<a name="SVG.Debug"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Debug](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L381>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Debug() []string
```

Debug returns the renderer's debug trace. No parameters are required. Returns a slice of debug messages.

<a name="SVG.Footer"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Footer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L396>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Footer(footers [][]string, ctx tw.Formatting)
```

Footer buffers footer lines for SVG rendering. Parameters include w \(w\), footers \(lines\), and ctx \(formatting\). No return value; stores data for later rendering.

<a name="SVG.Header"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Header](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L425>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Header(headers [][]string, ctx tw.Formatting)
```

Header buffers header lines for SVG rendering. Parameters include w \(w\), headers \(lines\), and ctx \(formatting\). No return value; stores data for later rendering.

<a name="SVG.Line"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Line](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L437>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Line(ctx tw.Formatting)
```

Line handles border rendering \(ignored in SVG renderer\). Parameters include w \(w\) and ctx \(formatting\). No return value; SVG borders are drawn in Close.

<a name="SVG.Logger"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Logger](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L668>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Logger(logger *ll.Logger)
```

<a name="SVG.Reset"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Reset](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L644>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Reset()
```

Reset clears the renderer's internal state. No parameters are required. No return value; prepares for new rendering.

<a name="SVG.Row"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Row](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L663>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Row(rowLine []string, ctx tw.Formatting)
```

Row buffers a row line for SVG rendering. Parameters include w \(w\), rowLine \(cells\), and ctx \(formatting\). No return value; stores data for later rendering.

<a name="SVG.Start"></a>

### func \(\*SVG\) [Start](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L675>)

```go
func (s *SVG) Start(w io.Writer) error
```

Start initializes SVG rendering. Parameter w is the output w. Returns nil; prepares internal state.

<a name="SVGConfig"></a>

## type [SVGConfig](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/svg.go#L17-L36>)

SVGConfig holds configuration for the SVG renderer. Fields include font, colors, padding, and merge rendering options. Used to customize SVG output appearance and behavior.

```go
type SVGConfig struct {
    FontFamily              string  // e.g., "Arial, sans-serif"
    FontSize                float64 // Base font size in SVG units
    LineHeightFactor        float64 // Factor for line height (e.g., 1.2)
    Padding                 float64 // Padding inside cells
    StrokeWidth             float64 // Line width for borders
    StrokeColor             string  // Color for strokes (e.g., "black")
    HeaderBG                string  // Background color for header
    RowBG                   string  // Background color for rows
    RowAltBG                string  // Alternating row background color
    FooterBG                string  // Background color for footer
    HeaderColor             string  // Text color for header
    RowColor                string  // Text color for rows
    FooterColor             string  // Text color for footer
    ApproxCharWidthFactor   float64 // Char width relative to FontSize
    MinColWidth             float64 // Minimum column width
    RenderTWConfigOverrides bool    // Override SVG alignments with tablewriter
    Debug                   bool    // Enable debug logging
    ScaleFactor             float64 // Scaling factor for SVG
}
```

<a name="Tint"></a>

## type [Tint](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/tint.go#L9-L13>)

Tint defines foreground and background color settings for table elements, with optional per\-column overrides.

```go
type Tint struct {
    FG      Colors // Foreground color attributes
    BG      Colors // Background color attributes
    Columns []Tint // Per-column color settings
}
```

<a name="Tint.Apply"></a>

### func \(Tint\) [Apply](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/renderer/tint.go#L16>)

```go
func (t Tint) Apply(text string) string
```

Apply applies the Tint's foreground and background colors to the given text, returning the text unchanged if no colors are set.


<a name="MaskEmail"></a>

## func [MaskEmail](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tests/fn.go#L21>)

```go
func MaskEmail(cells []string) []string
```

MaskEmail masks email addresses in a slice of strings, replacing all but the first character of the local part with asterisks.

```go
func MaskPassword(cells []string) []string
```

MaskPassword masks strings that resemble passwords \(containing "pass" or 8\+ characters\) with asterisks.

# tw

```go
import "github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/tw"
```

Package tw provides utility functions for text formatting, width calculation, and string manipulation specifically tailored for table rendering, including handling ANSI escape codes and Unicode text.

Package tw defines types and constants for table formatting and configuration, including validation logic for various table properties.

## Constants

<a name="Pending"></a>Operation Status Constants Used to indicate the success or failure of operations

```go
const (
    Pending = 0  // Operation failed
    Fail    = -1 // Operation failed
    Success = 1  // Operation succeeded

    MinimumColumnWidth = 8

    DefaultCacheStringCapacity = 10 * 1024 // 10 KB
)
```

<a name="Empty"></a>

```go
const (
    Empty   = ""
    Skip    = ""
    Space   = " "
    NewLine = "\n"
    Column  = ":"
    Dash    = "-"
)
```

<a name="SectionHeader"></a>

```go
const (
    SectionHeader = "header"
    SectionRow    = "row"
    SectionFooter = "footer"
)
```

<a name="WrapNone"></a>Text Wrapping Constants Defines text wrapping behavior in table cells

```go
const (
    WrapNone     = iota // No wrapping
    WrapNormal          // Standard word wrapping
    WrapTruncate        // Truncate text with ellipsis
    WrapBreak           // Break words to fit
)
```

<a name="MergeNone"></a>

```go
const (
    MergeNone         = iota // No merging
    MergeVertical            // Merge cells vertically
    MergeHorizontal          // Merge cells horizontally
    MergeBoth                // Merge both vertically and horizontally
    MergeHierarchical        // Hierarchical merging
)
```

<a name="CharEllipsis"></a>Special Character Constants Defines special characters used in formatting

```go
const (
    CharEllipsis = "…" // Ellipsis character for truncation
    CharBreak    = "↩" // Break character for wrapping
)
```

## Variables

<a name="PaddingNone"></a>BorderNone defines a border configuration with all sides disabled.

```go
var (
    // PaddingNone represents explicitly empty padding (no spacing on any side)
    // Equivalent to Padding{Overwrite: true}
    PaddingNone    = Padding{Left: Empty, Right: Empty, Top: Empty, Bottom: Empty, Overwrite: true}
    BorderNone     = Border{Left: Off, Right: Off, Top: Off, Bottom: Off}
    LinesNone      = Lines{ShowTop: Off, ShowBottom: Off, ShowHeaderLine: Off, ShowFooterLine: Off}
    SeparatorsNone = Separators{ShowHeader: Off, ShowFooter: Off, BetweenRows: Off, BetweenColumns: Off}
)
```

<a name="PaddingDefault"></a>PaddingDefault represents standard single\-space padding on left/right Equivalent to Padding\{Left: " ", Right: " ", Overwrite: true\}

```go
var PaddingDefault = Padding{Left: " ", Right: " ", Overwrite: true}
```

<a name="Styles"></a>Styles maps BorderStyle to StyleName

```go
var Styles = map[BorderStyle]StyleName{
    StyleNone:        StyleNameNothing,
    StyleASCII:       StyleNameASCII,
    StyleLight:       StyleNameLight,
    StyleHeavy:       StyleNameHeavy,
    StyleDouble:      StyleNameDouble,
    StyleDoubleLong:  StyleNameDoubleLong,
    StyleLightHeavy:  StyleNameLightHeavy,
    StyleHeavyLight:  StyleNameHeavyLight,
    StyleLightDouble: StyleNameLightDouble,
    StyleDoubleLight: StyleNameDoubleLight,
    StyleRounded:     StyleNameRounded,
    StyleMarkdown:    StyleNameMarkdown,
    StyleGraphical:   StyleNameGraphical,
    StyleMerger:      StyleNameMerger,
    StyleDefault:     StyleNameLight,
    StyleDotted:      StyleNameDotted,
    StyleArrow:       StyleNameArrow,
    StyleStarry:      StyleNameStarry,
    StyleHearts:      StyleNameHearts,
    StyleCircuit:     StyleNameCircuit,
    StyleNature:      StyleNameNature,
    StyleArtistic:    StyleNameArtistic,
    Style8Bit:        StyleName8Bit,
    StyleChaos:       StyleNameChaos,
    StyleDots:        StyleNameDots,
    StyleBlocks:      StyleNameBlocks,
    StyleZen:         StyleNameZen,
    StyleVintage:     StyleNameVintage,
    StyleSketch:      StyleNameSketch,
    StyleArrowDouble: StyleNameArrowDouble,
    StyleCelestial:   StyleNameCelestial,
    StyleCyber:       StyleNameCyber,
    StyleRunic:       StyleNameRunic,
    StyleIndustrial:  StyleNameIndustrial,
    StyleInk:         StyleNameInk,
    StyleArcade:      StyleNameArcade,
    StyleBlossom:     StyleNameBlossom,
    StyleFrosted:     StyleNameFrosted,
    StyleMosaic:      StyleNameMosaic,
    StyleUFO:         StyleNameUFO,
    StyleSteampunk:   StyleNameSteampunk,
    StyleGalaxy:      StyleNameGalaxy,
    StyleJazz:        StyleNameJazz,
    StylePuzzle:      StyleNamePuzzle,
    StyleHypno:       StyleNameHypno,
}
```

<a name="BreakPoint"></a>

## func [BreakPoint](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L197>)

```go
func BreakPoint(s string, limit int) int
```

BreakPoint finds the rune index where the display width of a string first exceeds the specified limit. It returns the number of runes if the entire string fits, or 0 if nothing fits.

<a name="IsIsNumericOrSpace"></a>

## func [IsIsNumericOrSpace](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L103>)

```go
func IsIsNumericOrSpace(r rune) bool
```

IsIsNumericOrSpace checks if a rune is a digit or space character. Used in formatting logic to determine safe character replacements.

<a name="IsNumeric"></a>

## func [IsNumeric](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L108>)

```go
func IsNumeric(s string) bool
```

IsNumeric checks if a string represents a valid integer or floating\-point number.

<a name="Max"></a>

## func [Max](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L180>)

```go
func Max(a, b int) int
```

Max returns the greater of two integers.

<a name="Min"></a>

## func [Min](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L188>)

```go
func Min(a, b int) int
```

Min returns the smaller of two integers.

<a name="Or"></a>

## func [Or](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L172>)

```go
func Or(cond bool, valid, inValid string) string
```

Or provides a ternary\-like operation for strings, returning 'valid' if cond is true, else 'inValid'.

<a name="Pad"></a>

## func [Pad](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L84>)

```go
func Pad(s, padChar string, totalWidth int, alignment Align) string
```

Pad aligns a string within a specified width using a padding character. It truncates if the string is wider than the target width.

<a name="PadCenter"></a>

## func [PadCenter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L48>)

```go
func PadCenter(s, pad string, width int) string
```

PadCenter centers a string within a specified width using a padding character. Extra padding is split between left and right, with slight preference to left if uneven.

<a name="PadLeft"></a>

## func [PadLeft](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L72>)

```go
func PadLeft(s, pad string, width int) string
```

PadLeft right\-aligns a string within a specified width, filling remaining space on the left with padding.

<a name="PadRight"></a>

## func [PadRight](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L61>)

```go
func PadRight(s, pad string, width int) string
```

PadRight left\-aligns a string within a specified width, filling remaining space on the right with padding.

<a name="SplitCamelCase"></a>

## func [SplitCamelCase](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L124>)

```go
func SplitCamelCase(src string) (entries []string)
```

SplitCamelCase splits a camelCase or PascalCase or snake\_case string into separate words. It detects transitions between uppercase, lowercase, digits, and other characters.

<a name="Title"></a>

## func [Title](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/fn.go#L22>)

```go
func Title(name string) string
```

Title normalizes and uppercases a label string for use in headers. It replaces underscores and certain dots with spaces and trims whitespace.

<a name="Align"></a>

## type [Align](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L45>)

Align specifies the text alignment within a table cell.

```go
type Align string
```

<a name="AlignNone"></a>Table Alignment Constants Defines text alignment options for table content

```go
const (
    AlignNone    Align = "none"    // Center-aligned text
    AlignCenter  Align = "center"  // Center-aligned text
    AlignRight   Align = "right"   // Right-aligned text
    AlignLeft    Align = "left"    // Left-aligned text
    AlignDefault       = AlignLeft // Left-aligned text
)
```


<a name="Behavior"></a>

## type [Behavior](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L167-L182>)

Behavior defines settings that control table rendering behaviors, such as column visibility and content formatting.

```go
type Behavior struct {
    AutoHide  State // AutoHide determines whether empty columns are hidden. Ignored in streaming mode.
    TrimSpace State // TrimSpace determines trimming of leading and trailing spaces from cell content.
    TrimLine  State // TrimLine determines whether empty visual lines within a cell are collapsed.
    TrimTab   State // TrimTab determines trimming of leading and trailing tabs from cell content.

    Header Control // Header specifies control settings for the table header.
    Footer Control // Footer specifies control settings for the table footer.

    // Compact enables optimized width calculation for merged cells, such as in horizontal merges,
    // by systematically determining the most efficient width instead of scaling by the number of columns.
    Compact Compact

    // Structs contains settings for how struct data is processed.
    Structs Struct
}
```

<a name="Border"></a>

## type [Border](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L111-L117>)

Border defines the visibility states of table borders.

```go
type Border struct {
    Left      State // Left border visibility
    Right     State // Right border visibility
    Top       State // Top border visibility
    Bottom    State // Bottom border visibility
    Overwrite bool
}
```

<a name="CellAlignment"></a>

## type [CellAlignment](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L52-L55>)

CellAlignment defines alignment settings for table cells.

```go
type CellAlignment struct {
    Global    Align   // Default alignment applied to all cells
    PerColumn []Align // Column-specific alignment overrides
}
```

<a name="CellCallbacks"></a>

## type [CellCallbacks](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L46-L49>)

CellCallbacks holds callback functions for cell processing. Note: These are currently placeholders and not fully implemented.

```go
type CellCallbacks struct {
    Global    func()   // Global callback applied to all cells
    PerColumn []func() // Column-specific callbacks
}
```

<a name="CellConfig"></a>

## type [CellConfig](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L58-L70>)

CellConfig combines formatting, padding, and callback settings for a table section.

```go
type CellConfig struct {
    Formatting   CellFormatting // Cell formatting options
    Padding      CellPadding    // Padding configuration
    Callbacks    CellCallbacks  // Callback functions (unused)
    Filter       CellFilter     // Function to filter cell content (renamed from Filter Filter)
    Alignment    CellAlignment  // Alignment configuration for cells
    ColMaxWidths CellWidth      // Per-column maximum width overrides
    Merging      CellMerging    // Merging holds all configuration related to cell merging.

    // Deprecated: use Alignment.PerColumn instead. Will be removed in a future version.
    // will be removed soon
    ColumnAligns []Align // Per-column alignment overrides
}
```

<a name="CellContext"></a>

## type [CellContext](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L47-L53>)

CellContext defines the properties and formatting state of an individual table cell.

```go
type CellContext struct {
    Data    string     // Content to be displayed in the cell, provided by the caller
    Align   Align      // Text alignment within the cell (Left, Right, Center, Skip)
    Padding Padding    // Padding characters surrounding the cell content
    Width   int        // Suggested width (often overridden by Row.Widths)
    Merge   MergeState // Details about cell spanning across rows or columns
}
```

<a name="CellFilter"></a>

## type [CellFilter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L39-L42>)

CellFilter defines filtering functions for cell content.

```go
type CellFilter struct {
    Global    func([]string) []string // Processes the entire row
    PerColumn []func(string) string   // Processes individual cells by column
}
```

<a name="CellFormatting"></a>

## type [CellFormatting](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L4-L15>)

CellFormatting holds formatting options for table cells.

```go
type CellFormatting struct {
    AutoWrap   int   // Wrapping behavior (e.g., WrapTruncate, WrapNormal)
    AutoFormat State // Enables automatic formatting (e.g., title case for headers)

    // Deprecated: Kept for backward compatibility. Use CellConfig.CellMerging.Mode instead.
    // This will be removed in a future version.
    MergeMode int

    // Deprecated: Kept for backward compatibility. Use CellConfig.Alignment instead.
    // This will be removed in a future version.
    Alignment Align
}
```

<a name="CellMerging"></a>

## type [CellMerging](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L19-L30>)

CellMerging holds the configuration for how cells should be merged. This new struct replaces the deprecated MergeMode.

```go
type CellMerging struct {
    // Mode is a bitmask specifying the type of merge (e.g., MergeHorizontal, MergeVertical).
    Mode int

    // ByColumnIndex specifies which column indices should be considered for merging.
    // If the mapper is nil or empty, merging applies to all columns (if Mode is set).
    // Otherwise, only columns with an index present as a key will be merged.
    ByColumnIndex Mapper[int, bool]

    // ByRowIndex is reserved for future features to specify merging on specific rows.
    ByRowIndex Mapper[int, bool]
}
```

<a name="CellPadding"></a>

## type [CellPadding](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L33-L36>)

CellPadding defines padding settings for table cells.

```go
type CellPadding struct {
    Global    Padding   // Default padding applied to all cells
    PerColumn []Padding // Column-specific padding overrides
}
```

<a name="CellWidth"></a>

## type [CellWidth](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/cell.go#L72-L75>)

```go
type CellWidth struct {
    Global    int
    PerColumn Mapper[int, int]
}
```

```go
type Compact struct {

}
```

<a name="Counter"></a>

## type [Counter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L39-L42>)

Counter defines an interface that combines io.Writer with a method to retrieve a total. This is used by the WithCounter option to allow for counting lines, bytes, etc.

```go
type Counter interface {
    io.Writer // It must be a writer to be used in io.MultiWriter.
    Total() int
}
```

<a name="Filter"></a>

## type [Filter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L29>)

Filter defines a function type for processing cell content. It takes a slice of strings \(representing cell data\) and returns a processed slice.

```go
type Filter func([]string) []string
```

<a name="Formatter"></a>

## type [Formatter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L33-L35>)

Formatter defines an interface for types that can format themselves into a string. Used for custom formatting of table cell content.

```go
type Formatter interface {
    Format() string // Returns the formatted string representation
}
```

<a name="Formatting"></a>

## type [Formatting](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L38-L44>)

Formatting encapsulates the complete formatting context for a table row. It provides all necessary information to render a row correctly within the table structure.

```go
type Formatting struct {
    Row              RowContext // Detailed configuration for the row and its cells
    Level            Level      // Hierarchical level (Header, Body, Footer) affecting line drawing
    HasFooter        bool       // Indicates if the table includes a footer section
    IsSubRow         bool       // Marks this as a continuation or padding line in multi-line rows
    NormalizedWidths Mapper[int, int]
}
```

```go
type Level int
```

LineCounter is the default implementation of the Counter interface. It counts the number of newline characters written to it.

```go
type LineCounter struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}

```

Lines manages the visibility of table boundary lines.

```go
type Lines struct {

    ShowHeaderLine State // Header separator line visibility
    ShowFooterLine State // Footer separator line visibility
}
```

```go
type Location string
```

```go
)
```

<a name="Mapper"></a>

## type [Mapper](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L16>)

Mapper is a generic map type with comparable keys and any value type. It provides type\-safe operations on maps with additional convenience methods.

```go
type Mapper[K comparable, V any] map[K]V
```

<a name="NewBoolMapper"></a>

### func [NewBoolMapper](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L214>)

```go
func NewBoolMapper[K comparable](keys ...K) Mapper[K, bool]
```

<a name="NewIdentityMapper"></a>

### func [NewIdentityMapper](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L236>)

```go
func NewIdentityMapper[K comparable](keys ...K) Mapper[K, K]
```

<a name="NewIntMapper"></a>

### func [NewIntMapper](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L225>)

```go
func NewIntMapper[K comparable](keys ...K) Mapper[K, int]
```

<a name="NewMapper"></a>

### func [NewMapper](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L19>)

```go
func NewMapper[K comparable, V any]() Mapper[K, V]
```

NewMapper creates and returns a new initialized Mapper.

<a name="SlicerToMapper"></a>

### func [SlicerToMapper](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L125>)

```go
func SlicerToMapper[K comparable, V any](s Slicer[KeyValuePair[K, V]]) Mapper[K, V]
```

SlicerToMapper converts a Slicer of KeyValuePair to a Mapper.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Clear"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Clear](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L92>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Clear()
```

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Clone"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Clone](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L143>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Clone() Mapper[K, V]
```

Clone returns a shallow copy of the Mapper.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Delete"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Delete](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L54>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Delete(key K) Mapper[K, V]
```

Delete removes the specified key from the map. Does nothing if the key doesn't exist or the map is nil.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Each"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Each](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L116>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Each(fn func(K, V))
```

Each iterates over each key\-value pair in the map and calls the provided function. Does nothing if the map is nil.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Filter"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Filter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L123>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Filter(fn func(K, V) bool) Mapper[K, V]
```

Filter returns a new Mapper containing only the key\-value pairs that satisfy the predicate.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Get"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L25>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Get(key K) V
```

Get returns the value associated with the key. If the key doesn't exist or the map is nil, it returns the zero value for the value type.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Has"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Has](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L62>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Has(key K) bool
```

Has returns true if the key exists in the map, false otherwise.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Keys"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Keys](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L81>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Keys() []K
```

Keys returns a slice containing all keys in the map. Returns nil if the map is nil or empty.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Len"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Len](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L72>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of elements in the map. Returns 0 if the map is nil.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].MapValues"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [MapValues](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L134>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) MapValues(fn func(V) V) Mapper[K, V]
```

MapValues returns a new Mapper with the same keys but values transformed by the provided function.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].OK"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [OK](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L34>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) OK(key K) (V, bool)
```

OK returns the value associated with the key and a boolean indicating whether the key exists.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Set"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Set](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L45>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Set(key K, value V) Mapper[K, V]
```

Set sets the value for the specified key. Does nothing if the map is nil.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Slicer"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Slicer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L152>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Slicer() Slicer[KeyValuePair[K, V]]
```

Slicer converts the Mapper to a Slicer of key\-value pairs.

<a name="Mapper[K, V].SortedKeys"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [SortedKeys](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L163>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) SortedKeys() []K
```

<a name="Mapper[K, V].Values"></a>

### func \(Mapper\[K, V\]\) [Values](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/mapper.go#L103>)

```go
func (m Mapper[K, V]) Values() []V
```

Values returns a slice containing all values in the map. Returns nil if the map is nil or empty.

<a name="MergeState"></a>

## type [MergeState](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L56-L60>)

MergeState captures how a cell merges across different directions.

```go
type MergeState struct {
    Vertical     MergeStateOption // Properties for vertical merging (across rows)
    Horizontal   MergeStateOption // Properties for horizontal merging (across columns)
    Hierarchical MergeStateOption // Properties for nested/hierarchical merging
}
```

<a name="MergeStateOption"></a>

## type [MergeStateOption](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L63-L68>)

MergeStateOption represents common attributes for merging in a specific direction.

```go
type MergeStateOption struct {
    Present bool // True if this merge direction is active
    Span    int  // Number of cells this merge spans
    Start   bool // True if this cell is the starting point of the merge
    End     bool // True if this cell is the ending point of the merge
}
```

<a name="Padding"></a>

## type [Padding](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L186-L197>)

Padding defines the spacing characters around cell content in all four directions. A zero\-value Padding struct will use the table's default padding unless Overwrite is true.

```go
type Padding struct {
    Left   string
    Right  string
    Top    string
    Bottom string

    // Overwrite forces tablewriter to use this padding configuration exactly as specified,
    // even when empty. When false (default), empty Padding fields will inherit defaults.
    //
    // For explicit no-padding, use the PaddingNone constant instead of setting Overwrite.
    Overwrite bool
}
```

<a name="Padding.Empty"></a>

### func \(Padding\) [Empty](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L213>)

```go
func (p Padding) Empty() bool
```

Empty reports whether all padding strings are empty \(all fields == ""\). Note that an Empty padding may still take effect if Overwrite is true.

<a name="Padding.Equals"></a>

### func \(Padding\) [Equals](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L203>)

```go
func (p Padding) Equals(padding Padding) bool
```

Equals reports whether two Padding configurations are identical in all fields. This includes comparing the Overwrite flag as part of the equality check.

<a name="Padding.Paddable"></a>

### func \(Padding\) [Paddable](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L224>)

```go
func (p Padding) Paddable() bool
```

Paddable reports whether this Padding configuration should override existing padding. Returns true if either:

- Any padding string is non\-empty \(\!p.Empty\(\)\)
- Overwrite flag is true \(even with all strings empty\)

This is used internally during configuration merging to determine whether to apply the padding settings.

<a name="Position"></a>

## type [Position](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L15>)

Position defines where formatting applies in the table \(e.g., header, footer, or rows\).

```go
type Position string
```

<a name="Header"></a>

```go
const (
    Header Position = "header" // Table header section
    Row    Position = "row"    // Table row section
    Footer Position = "footer" // Table footer section
)
```

<a name="Position.Validate"></a>

### func \(Position\) [Validate](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L18>)

```go
func (pos Position) Validate() error
```

Validate checks if the Position is one of the allowed values: Header, Footer, or Row.

<a name="Renderer"></a>

## type [Renderer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L11-L20>)

Renderer defines the interface for rendering tables to an io.Writer. Implementations must handle headers, rows, footers, and separator lines.

```go
type Renderer interface {
    Start(w io.Writer) error
    Header(headers [][]string, ctx Formatting) // Renders table header
    Row(row []string, ctx Formatting)          // Renders a single row
    Footer(footers [][]string, ctx Formatting) // Renders table footer
    Line(ctx Formatting)                       // Renders separator line
    Config() Rendition                         // Returns renderer config
    Close() error                              // Gets Rendition form Blueprint
    Logger(logger *ll.Logger)                  // send logger to renderers
}
```

<a name="Rendition"></a>

## type [Rendition](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L23-L28>)

Rendition holds the configuration for the default renderer.

```go
type Rendition struct {
    Borders   Border   // Border visibility settings
    Symbols   Symbols  // Symbols used for table drawing
    Settings  Settings // Rendering behavior settings
    Streaming bool
}
```

<a name="Renditioning"></a>

## type [Renditioning](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L32-L34>)

Renditioning has a method to update its rendition. Let's define an optional interface for this.

```go
type Renditioning interface {
    Rendition(r Rendition)
}
```

<a name="RowContext"></a>

## type [RowContext](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L72-L80>)

RowContext manages layout properties and relationships for a row and its columns. It maintains state about the current row and its neighbors for proper rendering.

```go
type RowContext struct {
    Position     Position            // Section of the table (Header, Row, Footer)
    Location     Location            // Boundary position (First, Middle, End)
    Current      map[int]CellContext // Cells in this row, indexed by column
    Previous     map[int]CellContext // Cells from the row above; nil if none
    Next         map[int]CellContext // Cells from the row below; nil if none
    Widths       Mapper[int, int]    // Computed widths for each column
    ColMaxWidths CellWidth           // Maximum allowed width per column
}
```

<a name="RowContext.GetCell"></a>

### func \(RowContext\) [GetCell](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L82>)

```go
func (r RowContext) GetCell(col int) CellContext
```

<a name="Separators"></a>

## type [Separators](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L87-L92>)

Separators controls the visibility of separators in the table.

```go
type Separators struct {
    ShowHeader     State // Controls header separator visibility
    ShowFooter     State // Controls footer separator visibility
    BetweenRows    State // Determines if lines appear between rows
    BetweenColumns State // Determines if separators appear between columns
}
```

<a name="Settings"></a>

## type [Settings](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L103-L108>)

Settings holds configuration preferences for rendering behavior.

```go
type Settings struct {
    Separators  Separators // Separator visibility settings
    Lines       Lines      // Line visibility settings
    CompactMode State      // Reserved for future compact rendering (unused)

}
```

<a name="Slicer"></a>

## type [Slicer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L6>)

Slicer is a generic slice type that provides additional methods for slice manipulation.

```go
type Slicer[T any] []T
```

<a name="NewSlicer"></a>

### func [NewSlicer](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L9>)

```go
func NewSlicer[T any]() Slicer[T]
```

NewSlicer creates and returns a new initialized Slicer.

<a name="Slicer[T].Append"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Append](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L33>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Append(elements ...T) Slicer[T]
```

Append appends elements to the slice and returns the new slice.

<a name="Slicer[T].Clone"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Clone](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L116>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Clone() Slicer[T]
```

Clone returns a shallow copy of the Slicer.

<a name="Slicer[T].Contains"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Contains](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L100>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Contains(fn func(T) bool) bool
```

Contains returns true if the slice contains an element that satisfies the predicate.

<a name="Slicer[T].Each"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Each](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L73>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Each(fn func(T))
```

Each iterates over each element in the slice and calls the provided function. Does nothing if the slice is nil.

<a name="Slicer[T].Filter"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Filter](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L80>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Filter(fn func(T) bool) Slicer[T]
```

Filter returns a new Slicer containing only elements that satisfy the predicate.

<a name="Slicer[T].Find"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Find](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L105>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Find(fn func(T) bool) (T, bool)
```

Find returns the first element that satisfies the predicate, along with a boolean indicating if it was found.

<a name="Slicer[T].First"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [First](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L62>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) First() T
```

First returns the first element of the slice, or the zero value if empty.

<a name="Slicer[T].Get"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L15>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Get(index int) T
```

Get returns the element at the specified index. Returns the zero value if the index is out of bounds or the slice is nil.

<a name="Slicer[T].GetOK"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [GetOK](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L24>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) GetOK(index int) (T, bool)
```

GetOK returns the element at the specified index and a boolean indicating whether the index was valid.

<a name="Slicer[T].Has"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Has](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L57>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Has(index int) bool
```

Has returns true if the index exists in the slice.

<a name="Slicer[T].IsEmpty"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [IsEmpty](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L52>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) IsEmpty() bool
```

IsEmpty returns true if the slice is nil or has zero elements.

<a name="Slicer[T].Last"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Last](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L67>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Last() T
```

Last returns the last element of the slice, or the zero value if empty.

<a name="Slicer[T].Len"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Len](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L44>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of elements in the slice. Returns 0 if the slice is nil.

<a name="Slicer[T].Map"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Map](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L91>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Map(fn func(T) T) Slicer[T]
```

Map returns a new Slicer with each element transformed by the provided function.

<a name="Slicer[T].Prepend"></a>

### func \(Slicer\[T\]\) [Prepend](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/slicer.go#L38>)

```go
func (s Slicer[T]) Prepend(elements ...T) Slicer[T]
```

Prepend adds elements to the beginning of the slice and returns the new slice.

<a name="State"></a>

## type [State](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/state.go#L4>)

State represents an on/off state

```go
type State int
```

<a name="Unknown"></a>Feature State Constants Represents enabled/disabled states for features

```go
const (
    Unknown State = Pending // Feature is enabled
    On      State = Success // Feature is enabled
    Off     State = Fail    // Feature is disabled
)
```

<a name="State.Cond"></a>

### func \(State\) [Cond](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/state.go#L26>)

```go
func (o State) Cond(c func() bool) bool
```

Cond executes a condition if the state is enabled

```go
func (o State) Default() bool
```

Default checks if the state is unknown

<a name="State.Disabled"></a>

```go
func (o State) Disabled() bool
```

Disabled checks if the state is off

<a name="State.Enabled"></a>

### func \(State\) [Enabled](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/state.go#L9>)

```go
func (o State) Enabled() bool
```

Enabled checks if the state is on

<a name="State.Or"></a>

### func \(State\) [Or](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/state.go#L34>)

```go
func (o State) Or(c State) State
```

Or returns this state if enabled, else the provided state

<a name="State.String"></a>


String returns the string representation of the state

<a name="State.Toggle"></a>

```go
func (o State) Toggle() State
```

Toggle switches the state between on and off

<a name="StreamConfig"></a>

## type [StreamConfig](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/renderer.go#L119-L133>)

```go
type StreamConfig struct {
    Enable bool

    // StrictColumns, if true, causes Append() to return an error
    // in streaming mode if the number of cells in an appended row
    // does not match the established number of columns for the stream.
    // If false (default), rows with mismatched column counts will be
    // padded or truncated with a warning log.
    StrictColumns bool

    // Deprecated: Use top-level Config.Widths for streaming width control.
    // This field will be removed in a future version. It will be respected if
    // Config.Widths is not set and this field is.
    Widths CellWidth
}
```

<a name="Struct"></a>

## type [Struct](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/types.go#L155-L164>)

Struct holds settings for struct\-based operations like AutoHeader.

```go
type Struct struct {
    // AutoHeader automatically extracts and sets headers from struct fields when Bulk is called with a slice of structs.
    // Uses JSON tags if present, falls back to field names (title-cased). Skips unexported or json:"-" fields.
    // Enabled by default for convenience.
    AutoHeader State

    // Tags is a priority-ordered list of struct tag keys to check for header names.
    // The first tag found on a field will be used. Defaults to ["json", "db"].
    Tags []string
}
```

<a name="StyleName"></a>

## type [StyleName](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/symbols.go#L84>)

StyleName defines names for border styles

```go
type StyleName string
```

<a name="StyleNameNothing"></a>

```go
const (
    StyleNameNothing     StyleName = "nothing"
    StyleNameASCII       StyleName = "ascii"
    StyleNameLight       StyleName = "light"
    StyleNameHeavy       StyleName = "heavy"
    StyleNameDouble      StyleName = "double"
    StyleNameDoubleLong  StyleName = "doublelong"
    StyleNameLightHeavy  StyleName = "lightheavy"
    StyleNameHeavyLight  StyleName = "heavylight"
    StyleNameLightDouble StyleName = "lightdouble"
    StyleNameDoubleLight StyleName = "doublelight"
    StyleNameRounded     StyleName = "rounded"
    StyleNameMarkdown    StyleName = "markdown"
    StyleNameGraphical   StyleName = "graphical"
    StyleNameMerger      StyleName = "merger"
    StyleNameDotted      StyleName = "dotted"
    StyleNameArrow       StyleName = "arrow"
    StyleNameStarry      StyleName = "starry"
    StyleNameHearts      StyleName = "hearts"
    StyleNameCircuit     StyleName = "circuit" // Renamed from Tech
    StyleNameNature      StyleName = "nature"
    StyleNameArtistic    StyleName = "artistic"
    StyleName8Bit        StyleName = "8bit"
    StyleNameChaos       StyleName = "chaos"
    StyleNameDots        StyleName = "dots"
    StyleNameBlocks      StyleName = "blocks"
    StyleNameZen         StyleName = "zen"
    StyleNameVintage     StyleName = "vintage"
    StyleNameSketch      StyleName = "sketch"
    StyleNameArrowDouble StyleName = "arrowdouble"
    StyleNameCelestial   StyleName = "celestial"
    StyleNameCyber       StyleName = "cyber"
    StyleNameRunic       StyleName = "runic"
    StyleNameIndustrial  StyleName = "industrial"
    StyleNameInk         StyleName = "ink"
    StyleNameArcade      StyleName = "arcade"
    StyleNameBlossom     StyleName = "blossom"
    StyleNameFrosted     StyleName = "frosted"
    StyleNameMosaic      StyleName = "mosaic"
    StyleNameUFO         StyleName = "ufo"
    StyleNameSteampunk   StyleName = "steampunk"
    StyleNameGalaxy      StyleName = "galaxy"
    StyleNameJazz        StyleName = "jazz"
    StyleNamePuzzle      StyleName = "puzzle"
    StyleNameHypno       StyleName = "hypno"
)
```

<a name="Symbols"></a>

## type [Symbols](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/symbols.go#L6-L29>)

Symbols defines the interface for table border symbols

```go
type Symbols interface {
    // Name returns the style name
    Name() string

    // Basic component symbols
    Center() string // Junction symbol (where lines cross)
    Row() string    // Horizontal line symbol
    Column() string // Vertical line symbol

    // Corner and junction symbols
    TopLeft() string     // LevelHeader-left corner
    TopMid() string      // LevelHeader junction
    TopRight() string    // LevelHeader-right corner
    MidLeft() string     // Left junction
    MidRight() string    // Right junction
    BottomLeft() string  // LevelFooter-left corner
    BottomMid() string   // LevelFooter junction
    BottomRight() string // LevelFooter-right corner

    // Optional header-specific symbols
    HeaderLeft() string
    HeaderMid() string
    HeaderRight() string
}
```

<a name="NewSymbols"></a>

### func [NewSymbols](<https://github.com/olekukonko/tablewriter/blob/master/tw/symbols.go#L238>)

```go
func NewSymbols(style BorderStyle) Symbols
```

NewSymbols creates a new Symbols instance with the specified style
